summary:
The primary concern here involves potential intrauterine factors contributing to her late-term miscarriages; hence an assessment through hysteroscopic examination should be prioritized due its ability to visualize internal structures critical during this stage of gestation.
The recommended test focuses on identifying any specific anomalies within the uterus which might explain why she experienced these particular types of miscarriage events after initial development was established but before full term delivery became viable without intervention from external medical means. Hysteroscopy provides detailed insight into those areas not easily accessible via other imaging techniques alone making it essential given current knowledge regarding etiology behind similar cases reported clinically across various populations globally regardless socioeconomic status differences among them all sharing common risk profiles associated with certain obstetric complications including secondary infertility stemming from failed implantations occurring post-implantational stages requiring more invasive diagnostic procedures than initially suspected based solely upon physical exam results available prior initiating further workup steps necessary towards formulating comprehensive management plans tailored uniquely per individual needs present amongst diverse groups working diligently alongside their patients toward achieving optimal outcomes achievable under existing technological advancements currently utilized effectively treating complex fertility challenges
